# always-free-macro-calendar

> A Google Apps Script that automatically syncs global macro economic events to Google Calendar. Completely free — no paid APIs, no subscriptions.

## What it does

Tracks the following events and writes them to a Google Calendar, displayed in the user's chosen timezone:

- FOMC rate decisions and meeting minutes (Fed website + Finnhub API)
- BOE rate decisions (Bank of England website)
- ECB rate decisions (ECB website + hardcoded fallback)
- BOJ rate decisions (hardcoded; official page uses JS rendering)
- US CPI, PPI, Non-Farm Payroll, Unemployment Rate (Finnhub free tier)

Runs daily at 21:00 Taiwan time (UTC+8) via a self-rescheduling trigger. Syncs events up to 13 months ahead.

## Two ways to use it

**Option 1 — Subscribe (zero setup):**
Add the public Google Calendar directly. No account, no API key required.

iCal URL:
https://calendar.google.com/calendar/ical/55a4f43e580604a1dc84e794620385a01c4a127f30199bed4d3c66de4c87d5de%40group.calendar.google.com/public/basic.ics

Google Calendar add link:
https://calendar.google.com/calendar/r?cid=55a4f43e580604a1dc84e794620385a01c4a127f30199bed4d3c66de4c87d5de%40group.calendar.google.com

**Option 2 — Self-host (full control):**
Deploy `economic_calendar.gs` to your own Google Apps Script project. Requires a free Finnhub API key and two Script Properties (`FINNHUB_API_KEY`, `REMINDER_EMAIL`). Run `setupTrigger()` once to initialize.

## Key files

- `economic_calendar.gs` — main GAS script, single file, all logic included
- `appsscript.json` — GAS manifest (timezone: Asia/Taipei, V8 runtime)
- `CLAUDE.md` — architecture notes for AI agents working on this codebase
- `README.md` — full setup guide in English
- `README.zh-TW.md` — full setup guide in Traditional Chinese

## Why it is free

- Central bank websites (Fed, BOE, ECB) are scraped directly via HTTP
- US macro data (CPI/PPI/NFP) uses Finnhub's free API tier
- Google Apps Script and Google Calendar are free within standard quotas
- No third-party services, no ongoing costs

## Known limitations

- Finnhub free tier provides data ~1-2 weeks ahead; the daily trigger fills in events continuously
- ECB and BOJ annual schedules require a manual update each October-November (reminder email sent automatically on November 15)
- BLS.gov blocks GAS IP addresses; Finnhub is used as the alternative source

## Repository

https://github.com/yyu0310/always-free-macro-calendar
